chunk_summary: This section focuses on clinic workflows for consistent growth charting and documentation in preterm, LBW, and ELBW infants post-discharge, emphasizing corrected age, growth metrics, feeding plans, and follow-up schedules.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of corrected age in growth charting for preterm infants.
Learn how to interpret z-scores for weight, length, and head circumference in growth assessments.
Develop effective feeding plans for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants based on growth metrics and intake data.
Implement clinic workflows for consistent documentation and follow-up of growth progress.
key_facts: Corrected age is essential for accurate growth charting in preterm infants; example: 8 weeks chronological, 2 weeks corrected.
WHO growth charts are recommended for tracking growth using corrected age.
Z-scores for weight, length, and head circumference: -1.2/-1.0/-0.5, with weight showing a +0.3 z improvement since the last visit.
Feeding plan: 8 feeds/day, breastfeeding plus 20 mL expressed breast milk (EBM) top-up after each feed.
Follow-up plan: reweigh in 72 hours and reassess after 7 days.
reference_quote: Corrected age for preemies is critical for accurate growth tracking — American Academy of Pediatrics, HealthyChildren.
document_key_topics: Corrected age and growth charting for preterm infants
